body:
### Problem

**The most powerful cursor moving action - Binary cursor position**  

Hello, I have an idea for cursor position, I think its cool and very useful. I first shared this idea with [vim](https://github.com/vim/vim), now I want to share you again.

**The Biggest problem for me is cursor moving**  
I do believe that for many vim users who do not edit in English sometimes, how to  move the cursor to the target in horizontal direction is very very difficult for the reason that we are not able to use "f" to find where we want to go.  
1. `大家好，我是vim的狂热粉丝，很高兴在这里与大家相会！`
When my cursor at `大`, now I want to move to `高`, how can I get there quickly? I cannot use `f`,  Of course I can type `l` to go step one  by one, but what if the sentence is very very long?

2. `This is a very very very very very very very very very very very very long sentence.`
When cursor at `T`, how to go get last `v` quickly?



### Expected behavior


**Here is my idea**  
So I come up with an original way of cursor moving which depend on no languages - **The binary cursor moving**  
We binary move the cursor every time when type key maps, it will get the target very quickly without even thinking.  
And here is the code:  
```
" ==========binary position========== {{{
" This is the most powerful cursor moving action created by wonderful!!!
" As its name shows the cursor moving acts in binary mode
" <C-j> <C-k> binary move the cursor in vertical direction
" <C-h> <C-k> binary move the cursor in horizontal direction
" The cursor will always be in middle when first moving
" If you miss the target call <leader>c to clear the position and try again
" Also "h j k l" can reset the status too in normal mode just like <leader>c does
" The binary position is especially useful when edit with which is not English,
" beacuse it will be difficult to use f to find where you want to go

nnoremap <C-j> :<c-u>call <SID>BinaryPositionV("down", "null")<CR>
nnoremap <C-k> :<c-u>call <SID>BinaryPositionV("up", "null")<CR>
nnoremap <C-h> :<c-u>call <SID>BinaryPositionH("left", "null")<CR>
nnoremap <C-l> :<c-u>call <SID>BinaryPositionH("right", "null")<CR>

nnoremap v vmv
vnoremap <C-j> :<c-u>call <SID>BinaryPositionV("down", "visual")<CR>
vnoremap <C-k> :<c-u>call <SID>BinaryPositionV("up", "visual")<CR>
vnoremap <C-h> :<c-u>call <SID>BinaryPositionH("left", "visual")<CR>
vnoremap <C-l> :<c-u>call <SID>BinaryPositionH("right", "visual")<CR>

nnoremap <silent> j j: <c-u>call <SID>BinaryClear("true")<CR>
nnoremap <silent> k k: <c-u>call <SID>BinaryClear("true")<CR>
nnoremap <silent> l l: <c-u>call <SID>BinaryClear("true")<CR>
nnoremap <silent> h h: <c-u>call <SID>BinaryClear("true")<CR>
noremap <leader>c :<c-u>call <SID>BinaryClear("false")<CR>

function! s:ResetV()
    let g:v_beg = line('w0')
    let g:v_end = line('w$') + 1
    let g:v_mid = g:v_beg + (g:v_end - g:v_beg) / 2
    let g:v_last_p = 0
endfunction

function! s:ResetH()
    let g:h_beg = 1
    let g:h_end = col('$')
    let g:h_mid = g:h_beg + (g:h_end - g:h_beg) / 2
    let g:h_last_p = 0
endfunction

function! s:BinaryClear(quiet)
    call <SID>ResetV()
    call <SID>ResetH()
    if a:quiet !=? 'true'
        echom "Binary position has been cleared!"
    endif
endfunction

function! s:VisualMark()
    silent execute "normal! mw`vv`w"
endfunction

function! s:BinaryPositionV(direction, type)
    let v_p = line('w0')
    if v_p != g:v_last_p
       call <SID>ResetV()
       call cursor(g:v_mid, 0)
       let g:v_last_p = v_p
       if a:type ==? 'visual'
           call <SID>VisualMark()
       endif
       echom "call BinaryPositionV(\"" . a:direction . "\")" . " -> beg: " .  g:v_beg . " end: " . g:v_end . " mid: " . g:v_mid
       return
   endif

   if a:direction ==? 'down'
       let g:v_beg = g:v_mid
   elseif a:direction ==? 'up'
       let g:v_end = g:v_mid
   endif

   let g:v_mid = g:v_beg + (g:v_end - g:v_beg) / 2

   call cursor(g:v_mid, 0)

   if a:type ==? 'visual'
       call <SID>VisualMark()
   endif

   echom "call BinaryPositionV(\"" . a:direction . "\")" . " -> beg: " .  g:v_beg . " end: " . g:v_end . " mid: " . g:v_mid
endfunction

function! s:BinaryPositionH(direction, type)
    let h_p = line('.')
    if h_p != g:h_last_p
        call <SID>ResetH()
        call cursor(0, g:h_mid)
        let g:h_last_p = h_p
        if a:type ==? 'visual'
           call <SID>VisualMark()
        endif
        echom "call BinaryPositionH(\"" . a:direction . "\")" . " -> beg: " .  g:h_beg . " end: " . g:h_end . " mid: " . g:h_mid
        return
    endif

    if a:direction ==? 'left'
        let g:h_end = g:h_mid
    elseif a:direction ==? 'right'
        let g:h_beg = g:h_mid
    endif

    let g:h_mid = g:h_beg + (g:h_end - g:h_beg) / 2

    call cursor(0, g:h_mid)

    if a:type ==? 'visual'
       call <SID>VisualMark()
    endif

    echom "call BinaryPositionH(\"" . a:direction . "\")" . " -> beg: " .  g:h_beg . " end: " . g:h_end . " mid: " . g:h_mid
endfunction
" }}}
```

**Easy use:**
* `<C-j>`: when target below the cursor, move down
* `<C-k>`: when target above the cursor, move up
* `<C-h>`: when target on the left of the cursor, move left
* `<C-l>`: when target on the right of the cursor, move right

**How does it works(for the horizontal direction)**  
* We set three global variables: beg, end, and mid first.  
* when type \<C-h> or \<C-l> call a function
* if the cursor line has changed, then reset beg=begin of sentence, end=end of sentence, mid=mid of sentence. The cursor will be put in the middle at the first time
* if the target in the left of cursor we have typed \<C-h> then calculate in binary idea to put the cursor in the middle of the left side
* next if the target in the right of the cursor now we type \<C-l>, it will binary move to middle of the right side.
* when we type several \<C-h> \<C-l> we will closer to the target, then we can use "h" or "l" to move as usual.
> note: the same as virtical direction

**Visual select**
* v mode: type `v` then use \<C-h>、\<C-l>
* V mode: type `v` then use \<C-j>、\<C-k> and type `V` at last

**Why not create a plugin**  
As you can see, I think this is a very wonderful idea of cursor moving, also powerful of vim, I do believe many people need it and will be love of it. So I strongly recommended to apply this "Binary cursor position" in vim source code. I have never read vim source code and also have no time to do it. I hope all vim friends here can implement this idea someday. Also you can copy the code above to your vimrc directory if you like it, and note that it will cover the origin key map of \<C-j> \<C-k> \<C-h> \<C-l>.

comment_data.body:
In german language there are also characters, that are not in the ASCII Range: for example `**öäüß€ ...**`
I do not have any issues to jump to those multibyte/utf8 characters with `**ftFT**`
hjkl-movement also respects multibyte chars.


1) I can't reproduce your example in 1) because I do not have a keyboard with those chars. But I tested it with umlauts, which are valid charcodes in my keyboard layout, - jumping with multibyte chars works. Maybe there is an issue with your OS/Keyboard input method?

2) That is just a basic jump:
a) If you want the last `v`, just jump from the end to the start:
`$Fv`
b) Or find the first `v` and jump to the next ones:
`fv;;;;` ...
c) Or if you have the count of the `v's`
`12fv`

> Why not create a plugin

I think you do need to create a plugin, to see if it will become popular among CJK people. Once it is, it makes sense to consider upstreaming it to Vim/Neovim. Many other Neovim features like LSP, editorconfig also start as external plugins first

title: Binary cursor position

type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality
type.name: Enhancement
